jokes.text: I'm gonna get 12 eggs and part of a dead animal. Dealer's choice. Please and thank you.
jokes.setup: Normal breakfast order taking at a restaurant
jokes.punchline: Leslie's characteristically intense approach to ordering breakfast in overly technical terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her manic intensity applied to mundane tasks. The technical description of 'part of a dead animal' is both funny and reveals her directness.

jokes.text: Well, instead of getting swept up in my boyfriends' personalities, I am dating myself and trying new things. So every time I go out to eat, I order one thing that's typical me and then something that I would never order. Now I have two best friends, Ann and Ann. Each one more beautiful than the other.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining her new dating approach
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of having two best friends who are both herself, with the self-compliment about beauty
jokes.explanation: Clever concept with good character development for Ann. The 'each one more beautiful than the other' is a nice absurdist touch.

jokes.text: Which Ann is screaming like a maniac? Oatmeal Ann or catfish Ann? I'm not sure, because I instantly blacked out from extreme terror.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about Ann's skydiving experience
jokes.punchline: Ann maintaining the two-personality concept while revealing complete cowardice
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the setup, with the instant blackout being a relatable and funny character detail.

jokes.text: Yeah, we'll do a double date: You and you, and me and Ben.
jokes.setup: Suggesting ice skating together
jokes.punchline: Leslie treating Ann's self-dating as an actual double date scenario
jokes.explanation: Quick, efficient callback that maintains the running gag without overexplaining.

jokes.text: you are so brilliant and kind and stupid hot
jokes.setup: Leslie giving Ann a supportive pep talk
jokes.punchline: The unexpected casual insertion of 'stupid hot' in a list of sincere compliments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her enthusiasm breaking through formal speech patterns. The contrast between 'brilliant and kind' and 'stupid hot' is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Ann is giving up on love and deciding to have a baby with herself. And she has only been dating herself for six weeks. If she were dating a guy for six weeks, and they decided to have a kid, I'd be like, 'Congratulations, Ann...And Channing Tatum.' Because that is the only scenario that would make sense to me.
jokes.setup: Leslie's concern about Ann's decision timeline
jokes.punchline: The Channing Tatum reference as the only acceptable six-week baby scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent talking head with perfect Leslie logic - her standards are so high that only Channing Tatum would justify the timeline. Good escalation and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: What a gorgeous herbaceous medley. There's been a mistake. You've accidentally given me the food that my food eats.
jokes.setup: Ron being served a salad
jokes.punchline: His disgusted reaction phrased as polite confusion about getting animal feed
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Ron Swanson line. Perfect character integration with his anti-vegetable stance. The formal politeness contrasting with the insult is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Is a gerbil marrying a rabbit?
jokes.setup: Being told salad is traditional first course at weddings
jokes.punchline: Ron's confused question implying only small animals would want salad at their wedding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron line that maintains his character while being completely absurd. Maximum economy and perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Tom considers himself a foodie, which apparently means taking instagrams of food instead of eating it.
jokes.setup: Introducing Tom as a food expert
jokes.punchline: The accurate but dismissive description of modern foodie culture
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about social media food culture, delivered with Leslie's characteristic directness.

jokes.text: Caterer number one's presentation was simple, mm, yet exhausting. Number two's was subtle and provocative, like a coy Dutch woman guarding a dark secret.
jokes.setup: Tom giving his expert food opinions
jokes.punchline: Completely pretentious and nonsensical food criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character work - his tendency to overcomplicate and add unnecessary drama to simple things. The Dutch woman comparison is wonderfully random.

jokes.text: But number three's told a story-- a story from a book I wouldn't read but I would watch the movie of.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing his pretentious food review
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't read while maintaining pretentious tone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Tom's shallow approach to culture while maintaining his pretentious facade.

jokes.text: Oh, the mini-calzone? I wouldn't call it that. It was more like a savory pastry. Delicate little dough pocket filled with tomato sauce, cheese, and seasoned meat. Just a stunning culinary innovation. It was a calzone. It was literally just a small calzone.
jokes.setup: Tom describing an appetizer in overly elaborate terms
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt correction that it's literally just a small calzone
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Tom's pretentious description and Leslie's no-nonsense reality check. The escalation from fancy description to 'literally just a small calzone' works well.

jokes.text: No. Tragically, we are both heterosexual.
jokes.setup: Sperm bank worker asking if Leslie and Ann are a couple
jokes.punchline: Leslie's mock-tragic delivery about their sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with Leslie's characteristic dramatic flair applied to a mundane situation.

jokes.text: Some sperm. I meant in terms of a donor? Oh, you know, nice guy... Nice sperm.
jokes.setup: Worker asking what Ann is looking for in a donor
jokes.punchline: Ann's awkward literal interpretation followed by equally awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from Ann's social awkwardness in an uncomfortable situation.

jokes.text: Oh! This guy went to Harvard! So did the Unabomber.
jokes.setup: Ann excited about Harvard-educated donor
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate dark reality check
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure. Leslie's protective pessimism delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: This guys seems healthy. Loves cycling. Yeah, so did Lance Armstrong, and he turned out to be a lying drug user. Or a hero. I don't know where we stand on him now.
jokes.setup: Ann noting positive trait about cycling
jokes.punchline: Leslie's confused moral stance on Lance Armstrong
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about cultural confusion regarding Lance Armstrong, delivered with Leslie's characteristic uncertainty about complex moral issues.

jokes.text: He is the gentleman who was fired for emailing a picture of his penis to every woman in city hall. Guilty. Yeah, that's what the judge said.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining Joe's background
jokes.punchline: Joe's proud admission followed by legal confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Leslie's formal description to Joe's casual admission of guilt.

jokes.text: Free money, free porn. Best job I ever had. Also... If you're looking to buy some weed, I'm looking as well.
jokes.setup: Joe describing his donor experience
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate oversharing about drugs
jokes.explanation: Standard gross character moment. Predictable escalation of inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Babe, you look super hot. You realize you've said that about all of them? Well, she has, every single time. 'Cause she is super hot. Honestly, you'd look hot naked.
jokes.setup: Ben complimenting Leslie's outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Andy's escalating and inappropriate final comment
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate escalation with Andy's lack of filter, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Ugh. This suit makes me want to scold a Catholic child.
jokes.setup: April trying on Leslie's conservative suit
jokes.punchline: Her specific and darkly funny reaction to feeling authoritative
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted April line. Specific, dark, economical, and completely in character. The specificity of 'Catholic child' makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: I don't know who Ann Taylor is, but I hate her and I want to kill her.
jokes.setup: April continuing to react to conservative clothing
jokes.punchline: Her violent reaction to the clothing store brand
jokes.explanation: Good April character moment with her trademark violent overreaction to mundane things.

jokes.text: She had the patch sewn in. You can't remove it. What's in the pocket, you ask? A ticket stub from a Fleetwood Mac concert in 1995
jokes.setup: April examining Leslie's suit details
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and sentimental pocket contents
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Leslie's sentimental nature, delivered through April's discovery.

jokes.text: So tomorrow, I lead a public forum in Leslie's Fleetwood Mac sex pants. Fleetwood Mac Sex Pants. New band name. I call it. Ooh, you know what? Maybe just Fleetwood Mac.
jokes.setup: April describing her outfit choice
jokes.punchline: Andy's excitement over band names, then realizing Fleetwood Mac already exists
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment with his musician excitement overriding basic knowledge. 'Fleetwood Mac sex pants' is highly quotable.

jokes.text: Fleetwood Mac Sex Pants. New band name. I call it. Ooh, you know what? Maybe just Fleetwood Mac.
jokes.setup: April's 'Fleetwood Mac sex pants' line
jokes.punchline: Andy's dimwitted suggestion to use the already-existing band name
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy obliviousness that plays into his established character trait about not knowing famous bands.

jokes.text: Um, you don't want a demon baby.
jokes.setup: Ann asking about better sperm banks in Eagleton
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact warning about Eagleton genetics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie line combining her Eagleton hatred with deadpan delivery. Maximum economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: his favorite season of Friends, whether or not he hates jazz-- hopefully, he does
jokes.setup: Leslie listing important donor qualities
jokes.punchline: Her casual insertion of jazz hatred as a positive trait
jokes.explanation: Good character detail revealing Leslie's specific preferences delivered as universal truths.

jokes.text: I fully believe that a woman should be in charge of what happens to her body. In this case, the body is Ann's, and the woman in charge of it is me.
jokes.setup: Leslie stating progressive feminist principles
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself by claiming control over Ann
jokes.explanation: Excellently crafted joke with perfect structure and character integration. Leslie's controlling nature wrapped in progressive language is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: I don't mind the knuckle hair. I just think he's a dick.
jokes.setup: Discussing why Dave from HR was eliminated
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt assessment after seeming to defend him
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with maximum economy. The setup makes you think she's being charitable before the blunt assessment.

jokes.text: Small office. Mine's way bigger. The seats in my office are leather.
jokes.setup: Doctor being interviewed in Leslie's office
jokes.punchline: His immediately petty and competitive comments
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe character establishing petty, competitive personality through mundane details.

jokes.text: Guy's got a spinal fracture. He's not going anywhere.
jokes.setup: Ann suggesting he check on his patient
jokes.punchline: His callous joke about patient's inability to move
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing the doctor's poor bedside manner and establishing him as unsuitable.

jokes.text: Chris? I'm dying. I was dying earlier today. And then I died. Now I'm dead. I had to cancel a date with Shauna Malwae-Tweep. And I really like her. Do you think she'll still like me now that I'm dead?
jokes.setup: Chris being overdramatic about food poisoning
jokes.punchline: His completely serious concern about dating while 'dead'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - his extreme health consciousness creating dramatic overreaction to minor illness. The dating concern while 'dead' is absurdly logical for him.

jokes.text: I have voided more than Tom's body weight in the last 12 hours alone.
jokes.setup: Discussing the severity of food poisoning
jokes.punchline: Ben's specific and gross quantification using Tom as measurement
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with specific measurement that makes it more memorable and visual.

jokes.text: As Eleanor Roosevelt once said to Betty Ford... 'Hillary Clinton is great.'
jokes.setup: April attempting to sound official and knowledgeable
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical quote with wrong historical timeline
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd April moment showing her complete disregard for historical accuracy while trying to sound authoritative.

jokes.text: Friendship bands that I've made for each and every one of you. So now, I guess we're Park Pals. You should see the booger under this chair.
jokes.setup: April trying to create Leslie-style enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate gross discovery ruining the moment
jokes.explanation: Standard April/Andy dynamic with gross humor undermining April's attempts at professionalism.

jokes.text: When will women in this town stop scheming to get my sperm?
jokes.setup: Pete realizing what the interview is really about
jokes.punchline: His narcissistic assumption this happens frequently
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Pete's inflated ego and paranoia about his genetic value.

jokes.text: That son of a bitch is astute.
jokes.setup: Pete correctly guessing the sperm donor scheme
jokes.punchline: Leslie's grudging professional admiration
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie line showing her ability to appreciate competence even when it works against her. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: I know it's a winter's morn, but it feels like a summer's eve, 'cause 'the douche' is in the building.
jokes.setup: The Douche making his entrance
jokes.punchline: His pretentious weather metaphor to announce himself
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for The Douche - combines pretentiousness with narcissism in his signature style.

jokes.text: Oh, whoa! Smells like some vomit took a dump in here.
jokes.setup: Tom arriving at the office with food poisoning aftermath
jokes.punchline: Tom's typically crude but creative description
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate gross humor with creative metaphor structure.

jokes.text: I didn't eat those stupid mini-calzones. Haver-food rule number six-- never eat anything with a sauce I have to dip myself. Drizzle it on for me. I'm not your maid.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Tom didn't get food poisoning
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous food rule about sauce application and service expectations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment revealing his high-maintenance food preferences. The 'I'm not your maid' line is particularly good character work.

jokes.text: The calzones... betrayed me? Never again, guys. As God as my witness, they're dead to me.
jokes.setup: Ben realizing calzones caused the food poisoning
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration of eternal war against calzones
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment and callback to Ben's calzone love. The betrayal language and dramatic declaration are perfectly over-the-top for the situation.

jokes.text: I'm afraid this park will raise my taxes. Ah, well, fear not. Between the normal Parks budget and our corporate partners-- I'm also afraid the park will be noisy. And full of spiders. And dark at nighttime. I'm scared of a lot of stuff.
jokes.setup: Citizen expressing concerns about the park
jokes.punchline: Walter's escalating list of irrational fears
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of citizen concerns from reasonable to absurd, with Walter's honest admission of general fearfulness.

jokes.text: Is this gonna be a topless park? I don't think there are topless parks. Well, let's build the first one and be heroes.
jokes.setup: Harris asking inappropriate question about park features
jokes.punchline: His immediate pivot to enthusiastic leadership when told no such thing exists
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Harris's perverted enthusiasm and misguided sense of civic innovation.

jokes.text: Topless park. Topless park! Topless park! Topless park!
jokes.setup: Harris's suggestion gaining momentum
jokes.punchline: The crowd's enthusiastic chanting for inappropriate park feature
jokes.explanation: Standard mob mentality escalation showing how inappropriate ideas can gain traction in public forums.

jokes.text: Actually, a farting immigrant segment would be really funny. Ay dios... Good looking out, Leslie.
jokes.setup: Leslie sarcastically suggesting offensive radio content
jokes.punchline: Howard genuinely considering the offensive suggestion
jokes.explanation: Shows Howard's inappropriate professional instincts and Leslie's horror at being taken seriously.

jokes.text: I'm gonna scan a quick pic of them boobies for a little deposit in 'El banco de spanko.' That was the douche talking. Douche nation.
jokes.setup: Howard oscillating between his real and radio personas
jokes.punchline: His crude comment followed by the fake Spanish bank name
jokes.explanation: Standard character establishment for The Douche persona, though the fake Spanish is somewhat lazy humor.

jokes.text: You know, if we had a little girl, I would name her Elizabeth, after my grandmother. She was this strong, amazing woman. And if we had a boy, I don't know, I'd name him something funny, like 'Dick' or 'O. J.'
jokes.setup: Howard giving thoughtful answer about potential baby names
jokes.punchline: The jarring shift from sentimental to completely inappropriate name choices
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Howard's inability to maintain appropriate behavior even during sincere moments. The contrast between Elizabeth and 'Dick or O.J.' is well-crafted.

jokes.text: My God. I have driven Ann right into the belly of the douche.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizing her plan backfired
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic biblical/nautical metaphor about Ann's situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie dramatic overreaction with creative metaphor mixing biblical and Jonah references. Highly quotable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: So, Crazy Ira, remember that chick Ann I used to... Yeah, she was smoking hot! Turns out, she wants to have my baby. Anyway, her lesbo friend tells me she's on the prowl for some baby beans... And it's down to me and some other turd-munchers.
jokes.setup: Howard broadcasting about Ann's situation
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate public discussion with crude language
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor showing Howard's complete lack of discretion and professionalism. The crude language is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. I just wanted to toss my name into the ring, in respect to this Indian woman's vagina.
jokes.setup: Milton appearing unexpectedly at the office
jokes.punchline: His incredibly inappropriate and racist announcement
jokes.explanation: Standard shock humor from inappropriate character, but relies heavily on offensive content without much wit behind it.

jokes.text: I just wanted to toss my name into the ring, in respect to this Indian woman's vagina.
jokes.setup: Milton arriving at the office unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate and racist offer to be a donor
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe humor from a gross character, but not particularly clever beyond shock value.

jokes.text: Um, crappy. We only got four signatures. Two of them were me and Andy, and one of them said 'Farts McCool.'
jokes.setup: Ben asking about the forum results
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact description of the dismal turnout with fake name
jokes.explanation: Good April deadpan delivery with the absurd fake name providing the humor punch. Shows the forum's complete failure efficiently.

jokes.text: Thanks, JJ. You're the best.
jokes.setup: JJ serving food to the characters
jokes.punchline: The irony of praising JJ after his food nearly killed them
jokes.explanation: Good ironic callback showing the characters' short memory or politeness despite JJ's food nearly killing them.

jokes.text: 'Me so horny.' That was not me. That was a farcical sound effect. Did she say 'fartsicle'? The fartsicle. Frozen farts you can eat. Put it in your face-mouth! It's cold and it smells.
jokes.setup: Leslie being played on The Douche's radio show
jokes.punchline: The absurd fartsicle product concept with detailed description
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out radio humor with the escalating product description. Leslie's mortification adds character element.

jokes.text: We, uh, got an update on 'Operation... Baby douche.'
jokes.setup: The Douche giving a radio update
jokes.punchline: His name for the baby situation
jokes.explanation: Typical Douche branding of the situation with his name. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Okay, I will stop talking about your friend if you get in that Jell-o for one second and say, 'Rudy.' 'Cause The Cosby Show. Classic.
jokes.setup: Howard making a deal with Leslie
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific Cosby Show reference demand
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Howard's random pop culture obsessions and negotiating style. The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Rudy. There, happy? No, say it like Bill Cosby. Rudy.
jokes.setup: Leslie reluctantly complying with the deal
jokes.punchline: Howard's additional demand for accurate impersonation
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of demands showing Howard's manipulative nature and Leslie's growing frustration.

jokes.text: This bag is full of Fruit Roll-Ups and baseball cards. I don't have my Leslie pantsuit or my Leslie headband or my Leslie notes. I can't do this meeting without my Leslie stuff.
jokes.setup: April discovering Andy brought wrong bag
jokes.punchline: Her panic about missing Leslie costume elements and Andy's childish bag contents
jokes.explanation: Good character moments showing April's dependence on props and Andy's childish possessions. The panic is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: I didn't ask for a math lesson. So why don't you just tell me how much it's gonna cost me? And don't use numbers.
jokes.setup: April explaining park funding
jokes.punchline: Citizen's impossible demand for cost information without numbers
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with the impossible demand. The logic is so flawed it becomes brilliant, and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Look, you won't have to pay any extra money, okay? And don't ever call me 'ma'am' again.
jokes.setup: Citizen calling April 'ma'am'
jokes.punchline: April's threatening response to the polite address
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - her hatred of traditional politeness and authority. The threatening tone over 'ma'am' is very her.

jokes.text: Nobody wants your creepy stoner eyes staring at them while they're enjoying a park, okay? Stop being gross.
jokes.setup: Harris asking about topless park again
jokes.punchline: April's specific and brutal shutdown
jokes.explanation: Good April insult with specific imagery. The 'creepy stoner eyes' description is particularly effective and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Shut me down! You scare me, and I gotta say, I kinda dig that.
jokes.setup: April aggressively shutting down Harris
jokes.punchline: Harris's masochistic appreciation of being dominated
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Harris's inappropriate response to authority. The masochistic element adds uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: But I think you do need to take a little bit more time with this decision. Maybe employ a more methodical approach. One that involves color-coded binders.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizing to Ann for being controlling
jokes.punchline: Her immediate return to being controlling with organizational methods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - she can't help herself even while apologizing for being controlling. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Is that a drawing of my reproductive system saying 'Let's do this'? The Knope way involves a lot of uterine cartoons. What can I tell you? Besides, what is more cuter-us than your uterus?
jokes.setup: Ann discovering Leslie's planning materials
jokes.punchline: Leslie's pun on 'uterus' and 'cute' with characteristic enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment combining her love of organization, inappropriate enthusiasm, and terrible puns. The 'cuter-us' pun is both awful and perfect for her character.

jokes.text: Is that a drawing of my reproductive system saying 'Let's do this'? The Knope way involves a lot of uterine cartoons. Besides, what is more cuter-us than your uterus?
jokes.setup: Ann discovering Leslie's baby planning materials
jokes.punchline: Leslie's uterus pun and admission about her cartoon habits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun with 'cuter-us/uterus' and perfectly captures Leslie's weird organizational methods. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Let's make a baby together. You should phrase that differently.
jokes.setup: Leslie enthusiastically supporting Ann's decision
jokes.punchline: Ann's practical concern about the phrasing implications
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Leslie's enthusiasm creating unintended implications. Ann's practical response is perfect timing.

jokes.text: She was awesome. So smart, so hot, so mean. I wanted to bone her right there in that dirty church basement.
jokes.setup: Andy praising April's performance at the forum
jokes.punchline: His characteristically inappropriate and specific sexual comment
jokes.explanation: Standard Andy inappropriate oversharing. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: That's mine. I wanted that back. Well, frankly, you should have thought of that before we burned it.
jokes.setup: Leslie discovering they burned her suit
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact blame-shifting
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - destroying someone's property then blaming them for not preventing it. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Uh, we are burning this awful pantsuit. That's mine. I wanted that back. Well, frankly, you should have thought of that before we burned it.
jokes.setup: April celebrating by burning Leslie's suit
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact response to Leslie wanting it back
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan logic from April about the irreversibility of burning. Perfect end to her Leslie transformation.
